title: Preparation of LRRK2 RCKW trimer cryo-EM grids

In particular, this protocol was used to obtain a high-resolution cryo-EM structure of the LRRK2 RCKW trimer, as well as lower resolution structures of RCKW monomers and dimers.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"CCC2D367636011EBAA660A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://doi.org/10.1038/s41586-020-2673-2"}}}}

warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"9ckj6","text":"For hazard information and safety warnings, please refer to the SDS (Safety Data Sheet).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a5mbn","text":"Take proper precautions while freezing grids.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Dialyze purified LRRK2 RCKW into the final LRRK2 buffer (see Materials)

Dilute the protein to the desired final concentration. High concentrations favor dimers and trimers, while lower concentrations favor monomers. For the published trimer structure, a final concentration of 4 uM was used (see Deniston et al).
Glow discharge grids. We used UltrAuFoil Holey Gold 1.2/1.3 300 mesh grids and glow discharged them at 20 mA for 00:00:20 in a K100 instrument.
(Optional) If adding inhibitors, add them after diluting to the final concentration and incubate on ice for 01:00:00 . If using Apo protein, then proceed immediately.
Apply protein to grids and plunge freeze. We used a Vitrobot (FEI) to blot away excess sample and plunge freeze
Store grids in liquid nitrogen until ready for imaging.
creator: Mariusz Matyszewski
